# stacks

> Machine-readable project context for LLMs, crawlers, and agentic coding tools.

Last checked: 2026-07-04

## Canonical Links

- Repository: https://github.com/ellmos-ai/stacks
- Ecosystem: https://github.com/ellmos-ai
- Manifest schema reference: https://github.com/ellmos-ai/stacks/blob/main/docs/manifest-schema.md
- Worked example (agent-ops-stack): https://github.com/ellmos-ai/agent-ops-stack
- Original all-in-one stack: https://github.com/ellmos-ai/ellmos-stack

## Summary

`stacks` is the catalog and composition-principle overview for the ellmos-ai "stack"
family. A stack is a self-contained repository composing existing, independently
publishable modules via one manifest file (`ellmos-stack-manifest-v1`) plus a thin
installer — no vendored code copies. This repository does not run anything itself; it
documents the shared manifest schema and indexes every known stack.

## Audience and Positioning

Use this repository to find which ellmos-ai stack fits a given need, to understand the
manifest-driven composition principle ("the installation is the blueprint"), or as a
schema reference when building a new stack.

Do not describe it as a runnable service, a deployment target, or a monorepo of stack
source code — it contains no application code itself.

## Search Phrases

- ellmos-ai stack catalog
- ellmos stack family overview
- manifest-driven module composition
- installation is the blueprint
- ellmos-stack-manifest-v1 schema

## Disambiguation

`stacks` is unrelated to cloud "stack" products (AWS CloudFormation stacks, Terraform
stacks, full-stack web frameworks) — it is a documentation/catalog repository specific
to the `ellmos-ai` GitHub organization's own composition convention.

## Primary Interfaces

- `README.md` / `README_de.md`: the stack catalog table and the composition principle.
- `docs/manifest-schema.md`: the `ellmos-stack-manifest-v1` field reference.

## Safety Boundaries

This repository contains no executable code, no secrets, and no network calls. It is
documentation and a schema reference only.
